content: Cross-adapter helper modules belong in src/adapters/shared/, not in per-IDE dirs. Per-IDE dirs are reserved for files implementing the Adapter interface from src/adapters/types.ts and registered in src/adapters/registry.ts. Directories holding only utilities (like the former src/adapters/vscode/ with just paths.ts) are misclassifications and should be relocated to shared/. Naming uses kebab-case to match siblings (utils.ts, diff-utils.ts, marketplace-vscode.ts, vscode-paths.ts).

name: marketplace-vscode-win32-appdata-override
description: Cross-platform VS Code-family extension dir resolution: keep table-driven {darwin,linux,win32} paths, but honor process.env.APPDATA at resolution time on win32 for parity with src/adapters/shared/vscode-paths.ts. Use forward slashes in literals (path.join normalizes on Windows). Test platform mocking via Object.defineProperty(process, 'platform', { value: 'win32', configurable: true }) with beforeEach/afterEach restoration.

name: cursor-session-reader-sqlite
description: Cursor session reader uses bun:sqlite to read VS Code-style state.vscdb workspaceStorage blobs; composite session id <workspaceHash>:<tabId> with traversal-safe validation that allows exactly one ':' separator and rejects '..', null bytes, slashes, backslashes

name: session-reader-defensive-scaffold
description: When implementing a SessionReader for an adapter without an installed reference instance, follow codex-cli's JSONL pattern: scan a single directory under the tool's home, defensive parse (skip malformed/typeless lines), reject path traversal with /[/\\\0]|\.\./. Document storage-path assumption in a top-of-file comment so the format can be confirmed later without rewriting the contract.

name: vscode-session-reader-uses-shared-paths-helper
description: VS Code extension SessionReader: use resolveVSCodeExtensionStorage helper, not hardcoded paths. In tests, call the same helper to compute fixture paths so tests work cross-platform without process.platform skips.

content: Defensive chat-session parsing (ADR-0016): require requests[] array AND at least one recognised id/timestamp field before treating a JSON file as a session. This filters non-session JSON (settings, indexes, edit state) without enumerating them. Accept sessionId/id/guid, creationDate/lastMessageDate/startTimestamp, and string|{text}|{parts} request shapes / string|{value}|{content:{value}} response shapes.

name: windsurf-session-mirrors-cursor-sqlite
description: Windsurf is a VS Code fork with the same workspace-storage SQLite layout as Cursor: User/workspaceStorage/<hash>/state.vscdb with chat blobs in ItemTable under keys 'workbench.panel.aichat.view.aichat.chatdata' and 'cascade.chatdata'. Session id is composite '<workspace-hash>:<tabId>'. The Cursor session reader is a 1:1 template — only swap path segments (Cursor->Windsurf), CHAT_KEYS, and ADAPTER_NAME. All defensive helpers (isSafeSessionId, blobToText, extractTabs, bubblesToMessages) port unchanged.

name: roo-code-session-reader-task-dir-layout
description: Roo Code (Cline fork) stores each session as a task directory under <globalStorage>/tasks/<taskId>/ with api_conversation_history.json (primary) and ui_messages.json (fallback). Session ID == directory name (no need to extract from JSON). project filter falls back through record-level cwd → sibling task_metadata.json. Defensive parser accepts role normalization (user/human/ask, assistant/ai/say) and content shapes (string, [{text}], {message:{text}}). Pattern: mirror copilot/session.ts factory + resolveVSCodeExtensionStorage, but iterate task dirs instead of JSON files.

name: cline-session-reader-task-dirs
description: Cline (saoudrizwan.claude-dev) SessionReader pattern: per-task directories under <vscode-globalStorage>/tasks/<taskId>/, with three sibling JSONs (api_conversation_history, ui_messages, task_metadata). Distinct from the SQLite cursor/windsurf workspaceStorage pattern. Anthropic-style content blocks (text/tool_use/tool_result) are pre-pass collected by tool_use_id, then attached as ToolCall.output during the build pass; orphan tool_results emit as separate tool-role messages. Iterate ALL VS Code variants in BOTH listSessions and loadSession (not first-hit only), since users may have Cline installed under stable + Insiders simultaneously. taskId itself is a numeric ms-epoch string and is the third-tier startedAt fallback after ui_messages.ts and api file mtime.

name: gemini-cli-session-reader-rewind-semantics
description: Gemini CLI session JSONL files mix four record kinds: PartialMetadataRecord (initial line, sessionId/projectHash), MetadataUpdateRecord ({$set: {...}}), MessageRecord (id + type: user|gemini|info|warning|error), and RewindRecord ({$rewindTo: <messageId>}). Rewinds truncate the message stream from the target id onward — when reducing records, key by message id in a Map so rewinds can drop in insertion order. An unknown rewind target clears the stream (matches upstream loadConversationRecord behavior in chatRecordingService.ts).

content: Tests in test/mcp/server.test.ts using an adapter as a 'no session reader' fixture break when that adapter gains a sessionReader. As of 2026-05, no-sessionReader adapters: forgecode, kilo-code, kiro, cline, roo-code, amazon-q, continue. Prefer one of these and document the choice in a comment.
